faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.desc: You can find the most frequently asked questions about your legal processes and our transparent working principles here.

q1.q: In which legal fields do you provide specialized services?
q1.a: As MK Law, we provide services in a wide range. We provide services in 26 different areas of expertise, especially corporate company law, real estate law, family and divorce cases, criminal law, inheritance distribution and technology law (KVKK, Fintech).

q2.q: How does the first meeting take place and is it paid?
q2.a: After making an appointment by phone, WhatsApp or our website, we hold video meetings at our office in Istanbul or via online platforms. Our preliminary meetings where we provide general information about the process to our prospective clients are free of charge.

q3.q: How are legal consultancy fees determined?
q3.a: Our pricing policy is transparent. Consultancy fees vary according to the type of case, its complexity and the effort to be spent. Based on the minimum wage tariffs of the Union of Turkish Bar Associations and the Istanbul Bar Association, we clarify all costs before providing the service.

q4.q: How long will my case take to conclude?
q4.a: Case durations vary according to the workload of the court and the nature of the file. Local court processes take an average of 8-18 months. After each hearing, we inform our clients with written reports and meticulously follow the procedural procedures for the fastest conclusion of the process.

q5.q: Do you have continuous consultancy (advisory) services for companies?
q5.a: Yes, we provide continuous legal consultancy services to many domestic and foreign companies on a monthly basis. In this context, we provide 'boutique' solutions covering contract management, labor law compliance, KVKK audits and general corporate management processes.

q6.q: Can I benefit from legal aid (free lawyer)?
q6.a: Persons whose economic situation does not allow them to pay the lawyer's fee may request the appointment of a free lawyer by applying to the Bar Association (Legal Aid Office) of their province. We provide guidance services to applicants in this regard.
